description: §65.3.2 / §65.4.1 — a flat-declaration `#{}` (a direct child of a component root, the DOMINANT `#{}` corpus pattern) that references a `<theme>` token via the `@` sigil (`color: @brand`) compiles clean. The flat-inline `#{}`→`style=""` emission path (emit-html.ts → renderFlatDeclarationAsInlineStyle → renderDeclValue → lowerCssValueRefs) runs the SAME `@`-sigil lowering as the scoped-selector path: `@brand` lowers to `var(--brand)` and a bare value (`padding: 16px`) is untouched, with NO error. The emitted `style="color: var(--brand); padding: 16px;"` shape is asserted in the source unit test.
rationale: §65.4.1: the flat-inline `#{}` is the dominant corpus pattern; Wave-1 lowers `@`-tokens there too, not only in a component-scope selector block. A valid flat-inline theme reference fires none of the E-THEME-* / E-STYLE-* family. The codes-half proof that flat-inline token lowering is additive (new emission, no new diagnostics on valid input).
